edges.target: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Mutant FLNA expression in fibroma cells explains recurrent digital fibromata.
Tissue-specific mutant FLNA expression in digital fibroma cells explains inclusion body fibromatosis.
Dysregulated filamin A/connective-tissue biology disrupts skeletal development in the distal limbs.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Dysregulated elastin biology in skin lesions explains the pigmentary skin-defect component.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: The skin-lesion branch of the TOD mechanism accounts for hyperpigmented papules.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Distal skeletal dysplasia and connective-tissue involvement produce flexion contractures.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Abnormal digital ossification in TOD produces clinodactyly.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: The dermatologic component of TOD includes alopecia.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Abnormal connective-tissue development in TOD includes accessory oral frenula.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Generalized skeletal involvement in TOD can produce scoliosis.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Broader skeletal dysplasia in TOD can result in short stature.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

edges.description: Craniofacial connective-tissue and skeletal involvement in TOD can include hypertelorism.
edges.source: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

nodes.description: Multiple or accessory oral frenula are a frequent mucosal finding in TODPD, likely reflecting abnormal fibrous tissue proliferation mediated by the FLNA mutation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000191 | Accessory oral frenulum | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hair loss (alopecia) is a frequent dermatological feature of TODPD, reflecting ectodermal involvement by the mutated filamin A protein.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001596 | Alopecia | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Lateral deviation of one or more digits (clinodactyly) is a frequent skeletal feature of TODPD, reflecting the abnormal digital ossification in this condition.

evidence.snippet: HP:0030084 | Clinodactyly | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Skeletal dysplasia predominantly affecting the hands and feet, including brachydactyly and terminal bone abnormalities. Although most striking in hands and feet, skeletal involvement can be more generalized.

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: Terminal osseous dysplasia (TOD) is an X-linked dominant male-lethal disease characterized by skeletal dysplasia of the limbs

nodes.description: Mutated filamin A exerts effects through dysregulated elastin biology, with absent elastic fibers in skin lesions, explaining the connective tissue manifestations including skeletal dysplasia and pigmentary defects.
nodes.id: Dysregulated Elastin Biology and Connective Tissue Manifestations

evidence.reference_title: Anetoderma in a patient with terminal osseous dysplasia with pigmentary defects.
evidence.snippet: The finding of absent elastic fibers in the skin lesions suggests that mutated filamin A, in part, exerts its effects through dysregulated elastin biology

nodes.description: A single recurrent mutation (c.5217G>A) at the last nucleotide of exon 31 of FLNA activates a cryptic splice site, removing the last 48 nucleotides from exon 31.

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: The variant c.5217G>A was found in six unrelated cases (three families and three sporadic cases)

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: The variant activates a cryptic splice site, removing the last 48 nucleotides from exon 31. At the protein level, this results in a loss of 16 amino acids (p.Val1724_Thr1739del), predicted to remove a sequence at the surface of filamin repeat 15.

nodes.description: Joint contractures, particularly flexion contractures of the fingers and toes, are a frequent feature, contributing to the hand and foot deformities in TODPD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001371 | Flexion contracture | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Hyperpigmented papular skin lesions, listed as a very frequent feature of terminal osseous dysplasia-pigmentary defects syndrome. These represent the characteristic skin pigmentation abnormality in TODPD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0025473 | Hyperpigmented papule | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Increased distance between the orbits (hypertelorism) is an occasional craniofacial finding in TODPD, part of the variable craniofacial dysmorphism reported in some patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000316 | Hypertelorism | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Infantile digital fibromatosis (inclusion body fibromatosis) is a hallmark of TODPD. Fibromata containing characteristic cytoplasmic inclusion bodies arise on the fingers and toes shortly after birth and recur after excision.

evidence.snippet: HP:0025197 | Inclusion body fibromatosis | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Anetoderma in a patient with terminal osseous dysplasia with pigmentary defects.
evidence.snippet: Terminal osseous dysplasia with pigmentary defects (TODPD) is a rare, X-linked syndrome classically characterized by distal limb anomalies, pigmented skin defects of the face, and recurrent digital fibromas.

nodes.description: Due to nonrandom X chromosome inactivation, the mutant allele is not expressed in patient fibroblasts but is expressed in cultured fibroma cells. This tissue-specific expression pattern determines the range of phenotypic manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: RNA expression of the mutant allele was detected only in cultured fibroma cells obtained from 15-year-old surgically removed material.

nodes.description: Punched-out pigmentary abnormalities over the face and scalp, with discolored linear facial lesions. Skin biopsy reveals absent papillary dermal elastic fibers (anetoderma).

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: pigmentary defects of the skin

evidence.reference_title: Anetoderma in a patient with terminal osseous dysplasia with pigmentary defects.
evidence.snippet: deformities of the hands and feet, craniofacial abnormalities, and discolored, linear facial lesions

nodes.description: Recurrent fibromas of the digits with onset in female infancy. Surgically removed material shows expression of the mutant FLNA allele in fibroma cells.

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: recurrent digital fibroma with onset in female infancy

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: RNA expression of the mutant allele was detected only in cultured fibroma cells obtained from 15-year-old surgically removed material.

nodes.description: Spinal curvature (scoliosis) is an occasional skeletal feature of TODPD. Monitoring and bracing or surgical intervention may be required as in other FLNA-related disorders.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002650 | Scoliosis | Occasional (29-5%)
Monitoring, bracing, and surgical intervention as needed for scoliosis

nodes.description: Short stature is an occasional associated feature of TODPD, likely secondary to the broader skeletal dysplasia affecting limb growth.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004322 | Short stature | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: At the protein level, the splice site activation results in loss of 16 amino acids (p.Val1724_Thr1739del), predicted to remove a surface sequence of filamin repeat 15, producing a structurally altered filamin A.

evidence.reference_title: Terminal osseous dysplasia is caused by a single recurrent mutation in the FLNA gene.
evidence.snippet: At the protein level, this results in a loss of 16 amino acids (p.Val1724_Thr1739del), predicted to remove a sequence at the surface of filamin repeat 15.
